title: एकादशी व्रत कथा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री एकादशी व्रत कथा ॥
॥ पद्म पुराण अनुसार ॥

॥ एकादशी माहात्म्य ॥

युधिष्ठिर बोले — हे भगवन्! एकादशी व्रत का क्या माहात्म्य है? इसकी उत्पत्ति कैसे हुई? कृपया मुझे विस्तार से बताइये।

श्रीकृष्ण बोले — हे राजन्! एकादशी की उत्पत्ति की कथा सुनो।

॥ कथा प्रारम्भ — एकादशी की उत्पत्ति ॥

सत्ययुग में मुर नामक एक भयंकर दैत्य था। उसने देवताओं को पराजित कर स्वर्ग पर अधिकार कर लिया। सभी देवता भयभीत होकर भगवान विष्णु की शरण में गये।

भगवान विष्णु ने मुर दैत्य से युद्ध किया। वह युद्ध बहुत दिनों तक चला। एक समय भगवान विष्णु बद्रिकाश्रम की एक गुफा में विश्राम कर रहे थे। मुर दैत्य ने उन्हें सोते हुए देखकर मारना चाहा।

तभी भगवान विष्णु के शरीर से एक दिव्य कन्या प्रकट हुई। वह अत्यन्त तेजस्वी और शक्तिशाली थी। उसने मुर दैत्य से भीषण युद्ध किया और उसका वध कर दिया।

जब भगवान विष्णु जागे तो उन्होंने मुर दैत्य को मृत देखा। प्रसन्न होकर उन्होंने उस कन्या से कहा — "हे देवी! तुमने मेरे लिए मुर दैत्य का वध किया, अतः तुम 'एकादशी' नाम से विख्यात होओगी। जो मनुष्य तुम्हारे दिन (एकादशी तिथि) को व्रत करेगा, उसके सब पाप नष्ट होंगे और उसे मोक्ष प्राप्त होगा।"

तभी से एकादशी तिथि को भगवान विष्णु का व्रत करने की परम्परा प्रचलित हुई।

॥ एकादशी व्रत विधि ॥

दशमी के दिन सात्विक भोजन करें, रात्रि में शीघ्र सो जायें।
एकादशी के दिन ब्रह्ममुहूर्त में उठकर स्नान करें।
संकल्प लें — "मम सर्वपापक्षयपूर्वकं श्रीहरिप्रीतये एकादशीव्रतमहं करिष्ये।"
भगवान विष्णु का पूजन करें — तुलसी, पुष्प, चन्दन, धूप, दीप, नैवेद्य से।
निराहार व्रत रखें। जल भी न पियें तो उत्तम, अन्यथा जल या फलाहार।
रात्रि जागरण करें — भगवत् कथा, कीर्तन, नाम-जप में लगे रहें।
द्वादशी के दिन प्रातः स्नान-पूजन कर ब्राह्मण भोजन कराकर व्रत का पारण करें।

॥ एकादशी का महत्त्व ॥

भगवान कृष्ण ने कहा — हे अर्जुन! जो मनुष्य एकादशी का व्रत नहीं करता, वह महापापी है। एकादशी व्रत से पितरों को मुक्ति मिलती है, कुल का उद्धार होता है।

गंगा स्नान, काशी निवास, सूर्य ग्रहण दान — इन सबका फल एकादशी व्रत से प्राप्त होता है।

शुक्ल पक्ष और कृष्ण पक्ष — दोनों एकादशी करनी चाहिये।

"एकादश्यां न भुञ्जीत पक्षयोरुभयोरपि।"

विष्णु सहस्रनाम का पाठ, गीता पाठ, हरि कीर्तन — एकादशी के दिन ये सब पुण्यप्रद हैं।

॥ फल ॥

सर्व पाप नाश और मोक्ष प्राप्ति।
पितृ दोष शान्ति और कुल का उद्धार।
धन, स्वास्थ्य, सन्तान सुख की प्राप्ति।
भगवान विष्णु की परम कृपा।
वैकुण्ठ धाम की प्राप्ति।

॥ इति श्री एकादशी व्रत कथा सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ekadashi Vrat Katha ||
|| Padma Purana Anusara ||

Yudhishthira Bole — He Bhagavan! Ekadashi Vrata ka kya Mahatmya hai?

Shrikrishna Bole — He Rajana! Ekadashi ki utpatti ki katha suno.

Satyayuga mein Mura namaka eka bhayankar daitya tha. Usane devataon ko parajita kara svarga par adhikara kar liya. Sabhie devata Bhagavan Vishnu ki sharana mein gaye.

Bhagavan Vishnu ne Mura daitya se yuddha kiya. Eka samaya Bhagavan Vishnu Badrikashrama ki eka gupha mein vishrama kar rahe the. Mura daitya ne unhen sote hue dekhakar marna chaha.

Tabhi Bhagavan Vishnu ke sharira se eka divya kanya prakati hui. Usane Mura daitya ka vadha kar diya.

Bhagavan Vishnu ne kaha — "He Devi! Tum Ekadashi nama se vikhyata hoogi. Jo manushya tumhare dina vrata karega, usake saba papa nashta honge."

|| Ekadashi Vrata Vidhi ||

Dashami ke dina sattvika bhojana. Ekadashi ko Brahma-muhurta mein snan. Sankalpa, Bhagavan Vishnu ka pujana. Nirahar vrata. Ratri jagarana — Bhagavat katha, Hari-kirtana. Dvadashi ko prana karke vrata parana.

|| Iti Shri Ekadashi Vrat Katha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: एकादशी की उत्पत्ति पद्म पुराण में वर्णित है। सत्ययुग में मुर दैत्य ने स्वर्ग पर अधिकार किया। विष्णु युद्ध करते हुए विश्राम कर रहे थे, तब उनके शरीर से एक दिव्य कन्या प्रकट हुई जिसने मुर का वध किया — वही 'एकादशी' कहलाई। कृष्ण ने युधिष्ठिर को बताया कि एकादशी व्रत से सर्व पाप नाश, पितृ दोष शान्ति, कुल उद्धार, धन-स्वास्थ्य-सन्तान सुख और मोक्ष प्राप्त होता है। दशमी को सात्विक भोजन, एकादशी को निराहार व्रत, विष्णु पूजन, रात्रि जागरण, द्वादशी को पारण — यह विधि है।
benefits: Origin story of Ekadashi from Padma Purana — most important Vaishnava fast
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation (moksha) through vishnu's grace
Frees ancestors from bondage (pitru dosh shanti) and uplifts the entire family
Observed twice a month (Shukla and Krishna Paksha) — 24 Ekadashis per year
Invokes health, wealth, progeny, and ultimately vaikuntha (vishnu's divine abode)
Especially powerful when combined with Vishnu Sahasranama and Gita recitation
description: The Ekadashi Vrat Katha, known in Sanskrit as एकादशी व्रत कथा, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Padma Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to free ancestors from bondage (pitru dosh shanti) and uplift the entire family and grant health, wealth, progeny, and ultimately Vaikuntha (Vishnu's divine abode). It is also said to destroy all sins and grant liberation (moksha) through Vishnu's grace. Recitation is particularly auspicious during Nirjala Ekadashi, Devshayani Ekadashi, and Devuthani Ekadashi. At 18 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ekadashi Vrat Katha in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ekadashi Vrat Katha?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadashi Vrat Katha (एकादशी व्रत कथा) is the collective name for the sacred narratives associated with each of the 24 (or 26 in adhika maasa years) Ekadashi tithis observed annually in the Hindu calendar — each Ekadashi having its own distinct deity-aspect, name and katha drawn principally from the Padma Purana, Brahmavaivarta Purana, Vishnu Purana and Skanda Purana. Ekadashi (literally 'the eleventh') is the eleventh lunar day of each fortnight (one in shukla paksha, one in krishna paksha), universally regarded in the Vaishnava tradition as the day most beloved to Lord Vishnu — and the day on which fasting from grains and pulses, recitation of Vishnu's names, and study of the Ekadashi Vrat Katha are said to grant fruits exceeding even the great yajnas. The 24 Ekadashis include Vaikuntha, Nirjala, Devshayani, Devotthana, Putrada, Mokshada, Mohini, Apara, Pavitra, Indira and others. (Sources: Padma Purana Uttara Khanda; Brahmavaivarta Purana; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Ekadashi observed and why is it sacred to Lord Vishnu?
seoFaqs.answer: Ekadashi tithi is sacred to Lord Vishnu for several puranic and yogic reasons. (1) The Padma Purana narrates that the demoness Mura was created by Vishnu's tejas to fight a great asura, but Mura then attacked Vishnu himself; from Vishnu's body emerged a powerful eleven-faced shakti who slew Mura — that shakti, blessed by Vishnu, became the personified deity of Ekadashi, and Vishnu declared that whoever fasts on Ekadashi will receive moksha. (2) In yogic physiology Ekadashi corresponds to a specific lunar-solar configuration when the body holds excess apana-vayu and accumulated rajasic-tamasic toxins, which fasting purifies. (3) On Ekadashi all the sins of the world are said to take refuge in food-grains; eating grains on Ekadashi therefore consumes those sins. (4) Ekadashi is universally proclaimed in the Vaishnava puranas as the supreme tithi for Hari-bhakti, japa and study. (Sources: Padma Purana; Brahmavaivarta Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the food rules for the Ekadashi vrat — what is allowed and forbidden?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Ekadashi vrat rules prescribe: (1) STRICTLY FORBIDDEN — all grains (rice, wheat, barley, millets), all pulses and dals (chana, moong, urad, masoor, toor, rajma), salt (especially common salt; sendha namak/rock salt is allowed), oil (especially mustard oil; ghee is allowed), sour foods, onion, garlic, non-vegetarian food, alcohol, and tamasic items; (2) ALLOWED PHALAHAR — fresh fruits, milk, curd (in some traditions), nuts, dry fruits, sabudana (tapioca pearls), singhada (water-chestnut flour), kuttu (buckwheat), rajgira (amaranth), potato, sweet potato, makhana (fox-nut), cucumber, raw bananas; (3) RECOMMENDED — drinking only milk, fruits and water (phalahar) or remaining nirjala (without water) for the most rigorous; (4) Strict celibacy, no anger, no harsh speech, no daytime sleeping, no eating after sunset on Dashami evening or before sunrise on Dvadashi parana. (Sources: Hari Bhakti Vilasa; Padma Purana; Dharma Sindhu.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ekadashi parana — and why is its timing so important?
seoFaqs.answer: Parana is the formal breaking of the Ekadashi fast performed on the morning of Dvadashi (the twelfth lunar day immediately following Ekadashi). The timing of parana is governed by precise rules: (1) it must be done after sunrise on Dvadashi but within the Dvadashi tithi (before Dvadashi ends — failing which serious dosha results); (2) it must be done only after Hari-vasara (the first one-quarter of Dvadashi tithi) has passed; (3) it must specifically not coincide with Harivasara or with the late Dvadashi; (4) the first food after the fast should be a small amount of tulsi-jal followed by some sattvic food (tradition prescribes anna donated to Brahmins as the ideal first food); (5) parana should be preceded by Vishnu puja and donation. Performing parana at the wrong time is said to nullify the entire Ekadashi merit. (Sources: Hari Bhakti Vilasa; Nirnaya Sindhu; Dharma Sindhu.)

seoFaqs.question: Which are the most important Ekadashis among the 24?
seoFaqs.answer: While every Ekadashi is supremely sacred, certain Ekadashis are universally recognised as having special significance: (1) Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha Shukla — also called Mokshada Ekadashi in many regions) — the gateway of liberation, on which the Vaikuntha-dwara at Tirumala and Sri Rangam temples is opened; (2) Nirjala Ekadashi (Jyeshtha Shukla — also called Bhima Ekadashi) — observed without water, considered to grant the merit of all 24 Ekadashis combined; (3) Devshayani Ekadashi (Ashadha Shukla) — Vishnu enters yoga-nidra; (4) Devotthana / Prabodhini Ekadashi (Kartik Shukla) — Vishnu awakens and Tulsi Vivah is performed; (5) Mohini Ekadashi (Vaisakha Shukla) — Vishnu's Mohini-rupa katha; (6) Putrada Ekadashis (Pausha Shukla and Shravan Shukla) — for blessing of children; (7) Apara Ekadashi (Jyeshtha Krishna) — for destruction of the gravest sins; (8) Indira Ekadashi (Ashwin Krishna) — for liberation of ancestors. (Sources: Padma Purana; Brahmavaivarta Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ekadashi vrat bestow according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Padma Purana, Brahmavaivarta Purana and other Vaishnava scriptures enumerate manifold benefits of the Ekadashi vrat: complete destruction of all sins of countless lifetimes — including the gravest mahapatakas; bestowal of Vaikuntha-prapti (eternal residence in Vishnu's supreme abode) — the highest phala promised to every sincere Ekadashi observer; liberation of ancestors from preta-yoni and lower lokas (especially through Indira Ekadashi and Apara Ekadashi); fulfilment of all righteous worldly desires through Vishnu's grace; bestowal of children for the childless (especially through the two Putrada Ekadashis); marriage of unmarried daughters; cure of chronic diseases through the physical and pranic benefits of regular fasting; cultivation of indriya-jaya (mastery of the senses) — the supreme yogic phala of fasting; and the supreme phala of moksha that the Padma Purana declares to be guaranteed to the lifelong Ekadashi observer. (Sources: Padma Purana phalashruti; Brahmavaivarta Purana; Vaishnava bhakti tradition.)
